data.title: Inventor creates YAHNIQUE BRUSH (LJD-500) for better toilet cleaning; offers safe & sanitary solution below $50.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on September 19, 2025, an inventor from Ridge, N.Y. shared the inspiration behind creating the YAHNIQUE BRUSH. This innovative accessory aims to simplify cleaning underneath the toilet rim. The inventor highlighted that the design provides a safe, convenient, and sanitary option for this task. The brush is intended to offer a practical solution, making toilet cleaning more efficient.

data.title: Morse Micro's MM8108 Wi-Fi HaLow SoC wins 2025 EDGE Awards for IoT 2.0, enhancing connectivity for the next era.
data.description: In a press release dated September 19, 2025, Morse Micro, a prominent company specializing in Wi-Fi HaLow silicon solutions, unveiled its MM8108 Wi-Fi HaLow System-on-Chip (SoC) as the victor in the 2025 EDGE competition. This initiative signifies the onset of IoT 2.0, focusing on enhancing connectivity on a larger scale. With offices in Sydney and Irvine, Morse Micro stands out as a key player in advancing wireless technologies.

data.title: Owens Corning selects Prattville, Alabama for new shingle plant. Facility to produce 6M squares of laminate shingles/year.
data.description: Owens Corning, a prominent player in building products, has disclosed the selection of Prattville, Alabama for its latest shingle plant in the southeastern region of the United States. This move, part of a previously announced strategic investment, aims to bolster the company's Roofing manufacturing network. The new facility in Prattville will house a four-wide laminator that is anticipated to generate approximately six million squares of laminate shingles annually. This expansion signifies Owens Corning's commitment to enhancing its capacity and presence in the roofing sector.

data.title: Datavault AI CEO to speak at XRP Seoul 2025 event backed by Ripple, highlighting key innovations. #technology #crypto
data.description: A recent report has revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to its lowest level in five years, standing at 3.8%. This marks a significant decrease from the previous quarter, where the rate was 4.5%. The report also indicates that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in the past month, surpassing analysts' expectations. Additionally, the average hourly wage saw a slight increase of 2.5%, with the manufacturing sector experiencing the most growth. Despite these positive indicators, experts are cautious about the long-term sustainability of this trend due to ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

data.title: Darden Restaurants' Q1 sales beat but analysts cut price target, causing shares to tank. TD Cowen drops target to $200, BTIG to $225.
data.description: Trading under the ticker symbol DR on the New York Stock Exchange, Darden Restaurants Inc saw a significant drop in its shares during early trading on Friday following the release of disappointing first quarter results. Analyst projections revealed TD Cowen analyst Andrew Charles maintaining a Hold rating and lowering the price target from $235 to $200, while BTIG analyst Peter Saleh reaffirmed a Buy rating but reduced the price target from $235 to $225. The decline in the company's stock was attributed to "elevated Olive Garden expectations" and recent share price appreciation, as per TD Cowen analyst Andrew Charles. For more analyst stock ratings, additional details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Lennox (NYSE: LII) announces $1.30 quarterly dividend per share, payable on Oct 15, to stockholders as of Sept 30.
data.description: The board of directors of Lennox (NYSE: LII) based in Dallas, announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.30 per share of common stock on September 19, 2025. This dividend will be payable on October 15, 2025, to stockholders holding shares as of September 30, 2025. Lennox (NYSE: LII) is recognized as a prominent company in the industry.

data.title: Consumer Watchdog challenges Newsom's climate bill signing as sham; calls it a "breath of fresh air."
data.description: In Sacramento, California on September 19, 2025, a Consumer Alert video was released by Consumer Watchdog, cautioning consumers about the impending signing into law of a package of bills by Governor Newsom. His office referred to the legislation as "big beautiful" with the aim to decrease electricity expenses and bring stability. The specific numbers or details about the bills were not provided.

data.title: Inventor from East Northport, N.Y. develops C C TRACK smartphone holder/track assembly for filming videos.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on September 19, 2025, an inventor from East Northport, New York shared an innovative solution for tracking and capturing movement in high-quality videos using a smartphone. The inventor introduced the C C TRACK as a way to enhance filming capabilities. This moving design aims to improve video quality and provide more efficient tracking features. The invention is set to revolutionize the way individuals create videos with smartphones. No specific numbers were mentioned in the provided information.

data.title: Hong Kong's construction industry output to slow from 3.7% in 2024 to 0.7% in 2025 due to weak housing market, high inflation, high fiscal deficit. -ResearchAndMarkets.com
data.description: In Dublin, the latest report on the Hong Kong construction market, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, projects a decline in industry growth from 3.7% in 2024 to 0.7% in 2025. This deceleration is attributed to various factors including a sluggish housing market and rising inflation. The report provides insights into the sector-wise analysis, covering commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential segments up to 2029. This slowdown poses challenges for the industry's performance in the coming years.

data.title: Dwight secures funds to originate $1B in construction loans for multifamily properties nationwide, adding to $3B loans closed YTD.
data.description: Dwight Securities Management LLC, an investment adviser linked to Dwight Capital LLC and Dwight Mortgage Trust LLC, revealed their success in obtaining funding to provide construction loans totaling up to $1 billion for multifamily properties nationwide. This capital injection will enhance Dwight’s loan origination reach, adding to the $3 billion in loans already closed this year. This move showcases their growth and strengthens their servicing portfolio to $13 billion.
